page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB RAM-to-vCPU ratio. Powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, the instance allocates 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU architecture includes 32 MB of L3 cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AES, VAES, SHA_NI, BMI2, ADX, and FMA. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPUs, and features zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The AMD EPYC processor delivers strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% of stress-ng benchmarks. However, multi-core and multi-threaded execution is relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression. Memory bandwidth is average for large uncached blocks, measuring 46.82 GB/sec for reads and 27.55 GB/sec for writes, but falls into the bottom 25% for small cached blocks.

The instance exhibits lower performance in database and web serving workloads, with weak results in Passmark database operations, Redis SET operations at 4.44 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 5.54 GB/sec. For machine learning tasks, LLM inference speed using a small 135M model is average for text generation at 139.24 tokens per second, but weak for prompt processing. Qualitatively, the c2d-highmem-4 offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-heavy workloads that do not require high parallel processing power or local NVMe storage, making it suitable for memory-bound single-threaded tasks, text processing, and lightweight caching layers.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized instance configured with 4 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it utilizes a dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 2 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The instance lacks bundled local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, whereas multi-core performance, database operations, and Redis throughput remain in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is average for large uncached blocks but weak for small cached blocks. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive, single-threaded applications, text processing, and basic caching, but is less effective for highly parallelized workloads, database hosting, or high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, offering strong single-core performance but weak multi-core and database capabilities.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-4 features 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, delivering strong single-core but weak multi-core performance.
tagline: An x86 memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores, strong single-threaded performance, and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 4 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25%
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Weak multi-core, database, and Redis throughput benchmarks
Best suited for memory-bound, single-threaded workloads and text processing
